claim: Anthropic's official Slack pipeline routes @Claude mentions through intent detection, spawns a Claude Code session on claude.ai/code, posts progress updates back to the originating Slack thread, and offers View Session / Create PR action buttons on completion.
quote: Initiation: You @mention Claude with a coding request ... Session creation: A new Claude Code session is created on claude.ai/code ... Progress updates: Claude posts status updates to your Slack thread as work progresses ... Completion: ... action buttons ... 'View Session' ... 'Create PR'.

claim: AnandChowdhary's claude-code-slack-bot uses Cloudflare Workers + Hono + TypeScript: a Slack mention triggers a 👀 reaction and immediate GitHub Issue creation (satisfying the 3s ack), then a Cloudflare Queue named 'progress-checks' polls GitHub every 10s for new comments and mirrors them to the Slack thread until the [Create PR ➔] completion marker.
quote: Bot adds a 👀 reaction and immediately creates a GitHub issue ... A queue named 'progress-checks' handles monitoring ... Checks GitHub for updates every 10 seconds ... maintains issue-to-thread mappings in Cloudflare KV (with 30-day TTL).

claim: mpociot's claude-code-slack-bot is a TypeScript/Node 18+ bot using Slack Socket Mode (so signature verification reduces to a signing-secret handshake, no public URL) with a working-directory-manager that scopes context per DM/channel/thread and streams Claude SDK output by editing the same Slack message as it 'thinks'.
quote: The bot uses Socket Mode for real-time connections and implements signature verification through the signing secret ... working-directory-manager.ts tracks context per conversation type ... messages update as Claude thinks ... File uploads are temporarily processed and automatically cleaned up post-analysis.

claim: 41fred/claude-code-slack splits the system into a FastAPI worker on Railway (handles Slack HMAC-SHA256 signature verification and the 3s ack, writes task JSON files into a tasks/ directory in a GitHub repo) and a local macOS daemon that polls GitHub every 5s, runs `claude -p` in an isolated cloned workspace, and posts results — using GitHub itself as the queue.
quote: The worker validates every incoming request using 'HMAC-SHA256 signature' ... Worker writes JSON task file to tasks/ directory ... Daemon polls and detects new files every 5 seconds ... Claude operates within a 'separate clone' workspace directory ... Workspace changes trigger automatic Git commits and pushes after each task completes.

claim: MattKilmer/claude-autofix-bot uses Node 20 + TypeScript + Express on Railway with a webhook at /api/slack-events; it shells out to the Claude Code CLI for full agentic explore/edit/test, uses simple-git plus the GitHub API to push a semantic branch and open a PR, and supports image attachments by downloading Slack file URLs and feeding them to Claude.
quote: The bot receives Slack events via webhooks at /api/slack-events ... invokes 'Claude Code CLI' with 'full agentic capabilities (explore, edit, run tests)' ... simple-git for local operations and GitHub API integration ... 'Screenshot Support: Attach images for visual context—Claude analyzes them'.

claim: David Calvert's 'Hive Ix' SRE Slackbot is <2,000 lines of Go deployed via Helm on GCP/Kubernetes, calling Claude Sonnet 4.5 through Vertex AI Model Garden by literally exec-ing `claude -p prompt`, with MCP servers (Atlassian, Datadog) attached for incident-response context.
quote: Language: Go (less than 2,000 lines) ... Infrastructure: Kubernetes with Helm charts ... Claude integration: Via 'Vertex AI Model Garden' with Sonnet 4.5 ... cmd := exec.Command('claude', '-p', prompt) ... MCP servers (Atlassian, Datadog).

claim: takafu/slack-claude-bot uses Bolt JS in Socket Mode, spawns a Claude Code CLI child process per app_mention, and — instead of a separate response formatter — passes SLACK_BOT_TOKEN/CHANNEL/THREAD_TS into Claude's environment so Claude itself uses the Bash tool with curl to post progress, add :thinking_face: reactions, and reply in-thread; concurrency is naturally serialized because the CLI only runs one session at a time.
quote: Each Slack thread maintains its own Claude session context ... The bot spawns the Claude Code CLI as a child process ... Claude receives environment variables ... uses Bash tool to call Slack API directly (via curl) for posting messages, adding reactions ... Claude Code CLI can only run one session at a time.

claim: jeremylongshore/claude-code-slack-channel is a TypeScript Bolt+MCP-stdio bridge with three runtimes (Bun/Node/Docker) that enforces per-thread session isolation, hash-chained tamper-evident audit journals, and a five-layer prompt-injection defense — every inbound Slack message is gated by allowlists/channel opt-in/mention requirement before reaching Claude.
quote: WebSocket (Socket Mode — outbound only, no public URL) ... Every inbound message hits a gate. Ungated messages are silently dropped before reaching Claude ... 'five-layer prompt-injection defense' ... 'per-thread session isolation' ... Hash-chained audit journal.

claim: chenhg5/cc-connect is a Go message-broker that bridges local Claude Code (or Cursor/Gemini/Codex) to Slack/Discord/Telegram/Lark via Socket Mode so no public IP is needed, with streaming chunked replies, async-by-default lifecycle hooks, and attachment send-back of generated files.
quote: Socket Mode — no public IP needed ... 'Streaming / chunked replies' for Slack ... 'Lifecycle Event Hooks' feature includes 'Async by default, fail-open' ... 'Attachment send-back' functionality allows agents to return generated files back to chat.

claim: tomeraitz/claude-slack-bridge inverts the pattern: it is an MCP server that lets a running Claude Code task pause and ask a human in Slack via a daemon holding one Socket Mode WebSocket plus a Unix-domain-socket server (/tmp/slack-bridge.sock) that routes thread_ts replies to the blocked session — useful for human-in-the-loop steps inside a longer Claude run.
quote: the daemon (persistent Docker container) holds one Slack Socket Mode WebSocket connection and a Unix domain socket server that receives all Slack reply events and routes them to the correct waiting session ... session connects to /tmp/slack-bridge.sock and registers, then blocks without polling.

claim: ClaudeFluent's recipe is the canonical Bolt-JS pattern: import { App } from '@slack/bolt', run in Socket Mode for dev, call await ack() within 3s and respond() asynchronously, and format replies with Slack Block Kit JSON; deployment is suggested on Railway or Render.
quote: import { App } from '@slack/bolt' ... 'It should run in Socket Mode (no public URL needed for development)' ... 'Slack requires a response within 3 seconds. If your data query is slow, acknowledge immediately with ack() and then send the data as a follow-up using respond().' ... Slack Block Kit.
